articles.headline: Special Court Discharges Kejriwal, Sisodia, Kavitha, and 22 Others in Delhi Liquor Scam Case
articles.explainer:
• A special court in Delhi discharged Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal, former Deputy CM Manish Sisodia, BRS leader K. Kavitha, and 22 other individuals and entities.
• The discharge pertains to the ongoing investigation into alleged irregularities in the Delhi Excise Policy 2021-22.
• The court's decision comes after a detailed review of the evidence presented by the investigative agencies.
• This development is a significant turn in the high-profile case, impacting the political landscape and ongoing legal battles.
• The court noted insufficient prosecutable evidence to proceed with charges against the accused at this stage.

Ministries and Departments of the Government

mainsPoints: Discuss the implications of such judicial decisions on political accountability and public perception of corruption cases.
Examine the challenges faced by investigative agencies in securing convictions in complex financial crimes.
Analyze the role of the judiciary in upholding due process and ensuring fair trial, particularly in high-profile cases.
Evaluate the impact of policy decisions, such as the excise policy, on governance and potential for irregularities.

articles.trivia: The Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA) was enacted in 2002 to combat money laundering and confiscate property derived from or involved in money laundering.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5% Amidst Easing Inflation Concerns
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive review.
• This decision reflects the MPC's cautious approach, balancing inflation management with supporting economic growth.
• RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das highlighted easing retail inflation but emphasized the need to remain vigilant against potential external shocks.
• The central bank reiterated its commitment to gradually withdrawing accommodation to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Experts project continued stability in lending rates, offering relief to borrowers and aiding investment decisions.

Examine the interplay between monetary policy and fiscal policy in achieving national economic objectives.

articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in India in 2016 and is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) to contain inflation within the specified target level.

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Strategic Dialogue Concludes, Focus on Indo-Pacific Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Strategic Dialogue concluded with a joint statement emphasizing deeper cooperation across various sectors.
• Key discussions revolved around enhancing maritime security, connectivity projects, and trade facilitation in the Indo-Pacific region.
• Both sides stressed the importance of a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, adhering to international law, particularly UNCLOS.
• New initiatives were announced to boost digital cooperation, green economy transition, and cultural exchanges.
• The dialogue reaffirmed India's 'Act East' policy and ASEAN's centrality in the regional architecture.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite EOS-07
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched Earth Observation Satellite-07 (EOS-07) from Satish Dhawan Space Centre, Sriharikota.
• EOS-07, weighing approximately 2,200 kg, was launched aboard the GSLV Mk-III rocket, marking another successful mission for ISRO.
• The satellite is equipped with high-resolution imaging sensors for advanced monitoring of agricultural land, forest cover, and urban planning.
• It will also provide crucial data for disaster management, climate change studies, and water resource assessment.
• This launch reinforces India's capabilities in developing and deploying sophisticated space-based Earth observation systems.

Examine the applications of remote sensing technology in areas like disaster management, environmental monitoring, and resource mapping.

articles.imageDescription: A GSLV Mk-III rocket lifting off from the launchpad, with the EOS-07 satellite orbiting Earth in the background.
articles.trivia: ISRO's Mars Orbiter Mission (Mangalyaan) made India the first Asian nation to reach Martian orbit and the first nation in the world to do so in its first attempt.

articles.headline: India Unveils Accelerated Targets for National Green Hydrogen Mission
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy announced accelerated targets for the National Green Hydrogen Mission, aiming for faster adoption and production.
• The revised targets include increasing green hydrogen production capacity to 10 million metric tonnes per annum (MMTPA) by 2030.
• Emphasis is placed on developing indigenous manufacturing capabilities for electrolyzers and other components to reduce import dependence.
• The mission aims to attract significant investments, create jobs, and reduce India's reliance on fossil fuels, contributing to energy security.
• New policy incentives and pilot projects in hard-to-abate sectors like steel and fertilizers were also highlighted.

articles.headline: Debate Intensifies in Parliament on Potential Uniform Civil Code Implementation
articles.explainer:
• A vigorous debate took place in both houses of Parliament regarding the potential implementation of a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) across India.
• Members from various parties presented their arguments, focusing on issues of religious freedom, gender justice, and national unity.
• The Law Commission's recent consultations and recommendations were frequently cited during the discussions.
• The government reiterated its commitment to Article 44 of the Constitution, which calls for a UCC, while assuring protection of cultural diversity.
• The discussions highlighted the complex socio-political and legal challenges associated with introducing a UCC.

articles.headline: Union Government Launches 'Nari Shakti Utkarsh Yojana' for Women's Economic Empowerment
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government launched a new flagship scheme, 'Nari Shakti Utkarsh Yojana,' aimed at enhancing women's economic empowerment across the country.
• The scheme focuses on providing skill development, entrepreneurial training, and financial assistance to women, especially in rural and semi-urban areas.
• It integrates existing women-centric programs and introduces new components for digital literacy and market access.
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development will be the nodal agency, collaborating with state governments and NGOs.
• The initiative seeks to boost women's participation in the workforce and foster self-reliance.

articles.headline: India Boosts Border Infrastructure Along LAC Amidst Geopolitical Shifts
articles.explainer:
• India has significantly accelerated the development of critical border infrastructure along the Line of Actual Control (LAC) with China.
• The projects include new roads, bridges, tunnels, and advanced surveillance systems to enhance troop mobility and logistical support.
• This strategic push is in response to evolving geopolitical dynamics and persistent border challenges in the Himalayan region.
• The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) has been instrumental in executing these projects in challenging terrains and extreme weather conditions.
• The enhanced infrastructure aims to bolster India's defensive capabilities and ensure rapid deployment of forces.

articles.headline: G20 Finance Ministers Address Global Economic Challenges, Debt Relief
articles.explainer:
• Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors from G20 nations concluded their meeting, focusing on global economic stability and sustainable recovery.
• Key discussions included strategies to combat persistent inflation, strengthen supply chains, and mitigate risks from geopolitical tensions.
• A significant part of the agenda was dedicated to enhancing debt relief mechanisms for vulnerable low-income countries.
• India, as a prominent G20 member, emphasized the need for coordinated global action on climate finance and digital public infrastructure.
• The meeting outcomes are expected to inform broader G20 leaders' summit decisions later in the year.

articles.headline: India Achieves Milestone in Domestic Semiconductor Production
articles.explainer:
• India's efforts to boost indigenous semiconductor manufacturing capabilities have achieved a significant milestone with the inauguration of a new advanced fabrication unit.
• The facility, a joint venture with a leading global chipmaker, marks a crucial step towards reducing India's reliance on imported semiconductors.
• The move is part of the 'Make in India' and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiatives, aiming to establish India as a global hub for electronics manufacturing.
• This development is expected to bolster various sectors, including automotive, consumer electronics, and defence.
• Government incentives and policy support have played a pivotal role in attracting investments and fostering technological advancement.

articles.headline: New Report Highlights Alarming Rate of Himalayan Glacier Retreat
articles.explainer:
• A comprehensive report released by the Ministry of Earth Sciences revealed an alarming acceleration in the retreat of Himalayan glaciers.
• The study, based on satellite imagery and ground observations, indicates significant ice mass loss over the past two decades.
• This rapid melting poses severe threats to water security for millions living downstream and increases the risk of glacial lake outburst floods (GLOFs).
• Scientists attribute the accelerated retreat primarily to rising global temperatures and localized black carbon deposition.
• The report calls for urgent climate action and enhanced regional cooperation for disaster preparedness and adaptation strategies.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.

Examine the importance of international and regional cooperation in addressing transboundary environmental challenges like glacier melt.
articles.imageDescription: A photograph showing a rapidly melting Himalayan glacier with a prominent glacial lake forming at its base.
articles.trivia: The Himalayas are often referred to as the 'Third Pole' due to the vast amount of ice and snow they hold, second only to the Arctic and Antarctic regions.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Directs Stricter Implementation of Environmental Impact Assessments
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, mandated stricter implementation and oversight of Environmental Impact Assessments (EIAs) for developmental projects.
• The court emphasized the need for independent expert committees, public participation, and post-clearance monitoring to ensure environmental sustainability.
• The judgment came in response to a petition highlighting violations and lax enforcement of EIA norms in several industrial and infrastructure projects.
• This ruling aims to strengthen environmental governance and prevent ecological damage caused by unchecked development.
• It underscores the 'polluter pays' principle and the judiciary's role in protecting environmental rights.

Examine the role of judicial activism in upholding environmental rights and ensuring accountability of project proponents.

articles.trivia: The concept of Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) was introduced in India in 1976 but was made statutory under the Environment (Protection) Act of 1986.

articles.headline: New Phase of Clinical Trials Begins for India's Indigenous mRNA Vaccine Candidate
articles.explainer:
• India's leading biotechnology firm announced the commencement of Phase III clinical trials for its indigenously developed mRNA vaccine candidate.
• This vaccine is designed to target emerging viral strains, showcasing India's growing capabilities in advanced vaccine technology.
• The trials will involve thousands of volunteers across multiple sites, adhering to stringent international safety and efficacy protocols.
• The development is a significant step towards achieving vaccine self-reliance and enhancing public health preparedness against future pandemics.
• Government funding and regulatory support have been crucial in accelerating the research and development process.

articles.headline: India Records Robust FDI Inflows in Q3, Boosts Manufacturing Sector
articles.explainer:
• India witnessed robust Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) inflows during the third quarter of the fiscal year 2025-26, signaling investor confidence.
• The manufacturing sector, particularly electronics and automotive, emerged as a major beneficiary, attracting a significant portion of the investments.
• Government's 'Ease of Doing Business' reforms and Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes were cited as key drivers for the increased FDI.
• These inflows are expected to create employment opportunities, foster technological transfers, and boost overall economic growth.
• The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) released the provisional data, highlighting positive investment trends.

articles.headline: India Advances Proposal for 'Ancient University of Nalanda' as UNESCO World Heritage Site
articles.explainer:
• India has submitted an updated nomination dossier to UNESCO for the 'Ancient University of Nalanda' to be inscribed as a World Heritage Site.
• The proposal highlights Nalanda's historical significance as a major Buddhist monastic university and a center of learning in ancient India.
• The updated dossier addresses previous technical queries and provides comprehensive details on the site's Outstanding Universal Value (OUV).
• The Ministry of Culture and Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) are spearheading the efforts to secure this prestigious recognition.
• Inscription would boost cultural tourism, conservation efforts, and international recognition for India's rich heritage.

articles.headline: National Water Policy Draft Under Public Consultation for Sustainable Water Management
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti released the draft National Water Policy 2026 for public and stakeholder consultation, aiming for integrated water resource management.
• The new policy emphasizes a shift from water resource development to water resource management, focusing on conservation, efficiency, and equitable distribution.
• Key proposals include promoting water-use efficiency in agriculture, adopting nature-based solutions, and ensuring 'water security' for all.
• It also addresses challenges like inter-state water disputes, groundwater depletion, and the impact of climate change on water availability.
• Feedback from the public and experts will be incorporated to finalize the policy, expected to be a blueprint for India's water future.

articles.headline: Government Unveils Draft AI Governance Framework for Ethical Development
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) released a draft national framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) governance.
• The framework aims to ensure responsible and ethical development and deployment of AI technologies across various sectors in India.
• Key principles include transparency, accountability, data privacy, fairness, and human-centric design in AI systems.
• It proposes a multi-stakeholder approach involving government, industry, academia, and civil society for policy formulation and oversight.
• The initiative seeks to balance innovation with regulatory safeguards, positioning India as a leader in ethical AI.

articles.headline: Government Amends E-Waste Management Rules to Promote Circular Economy
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) announced amendments to the E-Waste Management Rules.
• The revised rules aim to strengthen the Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) regime and promote a circular economy for electronic waste.
• Key changes include mandatory targets for producers, incentives for formal recycling, and stricter penalties for non-compliance.
• The amendments also focus on enhancing collection mechanisms and ensuring environmentally sound management of e-waste.
• This move is critical for addressing the growing challenge of electronic waste and conserving valuable resources.

articles.headline: National Mission for Climate-Resilient Agriculture Records Significant Progress
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare announced significant progress under the National Mission for Sustainable Agriculture (NMSA), particularly its climate-resilient component.
• The mission has successfully implemented climate-smart agricultural practices in several vulnerable regions, benefiting thousands of farmers.
• Focus areas include drought-resistant crop varieties, efficient irrigation techniques, soil health management, and integrated farming systems.
• Capacity building for farmers and adoption of modern technologies are key pillars for enhancing agricultural productivity amidst changing climate patterns.
• The progress underscores India's commitment to ensuring food security and farmers' livelihoods in the face of climate change.

articles.headline: India Plans Expansion of Strategic Petroleum Reserves to Enhance Energy Security
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas announced plans for a significant expansion of India's Strategic Petroleum Reserves (SPR).
• The move aims to boost the country's crude oil storage capacity, providing a critical buffer against global supply disruptions and price volatility.
• New underground storage facilities are being identified and planned across different regions to enhance strategic depth and accessibility.
• This initiative is part of India's broader energy security strategy, reducing vulnerability to external geopolitical events.
• Public-private partnerships and international collaborations are being explored for financing and technological expertise.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.

articles.trivia: India is the world's third-largest consumer of oil, and its dependence on imports makes strategic reserves crucial for buffering against supply shocks.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New Initiatives to Boost Tourism Sector Revival Post-Pandemic
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism launched a new set of initiatives aimed at accelerating the revival and growth of India's tourism sector.
• The programs focus on promoting domestic tourism, enhancing digital infrastructure for tourists, and developing niche tourism products.
• Special emphasis is placed on sustainable and responsible tourism practices, including eco-tourism and cultural heritage trails.
• Financial incentives for hospitality businesses and skill development programs for tourism professionals were also announced.
• The initiatives seek to position India as a premier global tourist destination, contributing significantly to employment and foreign exchange earnings.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements regarding the Delhi Excise Policy Case and related judicial proceedings is/are correct?
1. A Special Court in Delhi discharged key political figures, including the Chief Minister, from the case.
2. The case primarily involved allegations of irregularities in the Delhi Excise Policy 2021-22.
3. The Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA) was invoked in the investigation.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. A special court discharged Arvind Kejriwal, Manish Sisodia, K. Kavitha, and others. The case relates to alleged irregularities in the Delhi Excise Policy 2021-22, and the PMLA was a key act under which investigations were conducted.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding India's National Green Hydrogen Mission:
1. The mission aims to achieve 10 million metric tonnes per annum (MMTPA) of green hydrogen production by 2030.
2. It primarily focuses on encouraging imports of electrolyzers to meet domestic demand quickly.
3. The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy is the nodal ministry for this mission.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The accelerated target is 10 MMTPA by 2030. Statement 2 is incorrect: The mission emphasizes developing indigenous manufacturing capabilities for electrolyzers, not relying on imports. Statement 3 is correct: The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy is the nodal agency.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) in India, consider the following statements:
1. Article 44 of the Indian Constitution directs the State to secure a UCC for its citizens.
2. UCC falls under the Fundamental Rights section of the Constitution.
3. Goa is the only Indian state that currently has a Uniform Civil Code.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct (Article 44). Statement 2 is incorrect; UCC is part of the Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSP), not Fundamental Rights. Statement 3 is correct (Goa has a form of UCC).

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is primarily responsible for road construction and maintenance in India's border areas?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) is a statutory body under the Ministry of Defence that develops and maintains road networks in India's border areas and friendly neighboring countries. NHAI is responsible for national highways, PWD for state roads, and the Indian Army Corps of Engineers for military engineering support.

prelimsMocks.question: What is the primary objective of India's Strategic Petroleum Reserves (SPR)?
options: (a) To export crude oil to friendly nations during global shortages.
(b) To store refined petroleum products for domestic distribution.
(c) To provide a buffer against global crude oil supply disruptions and price volatility.
(d) To facilitate the transition from fossil fuels to renewable energy sources.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) To provide a buffer against global crude oil supply disruptions and price volatility.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Strategic Petroleum Reserves are crude oil stockpiles maintained by governments to meet emergency needs, such as supply disruptions caused by geopolitical events, natural disasters, or major accidents. They are not for export, refined product storage, or energy transition directly, though they contribute to energy security during the transition.

mainsMocks.question: The discharge of high-profile individuals in corruption cases often raises questions about the efficacy of investigative agencies and the judicial process. Discuss challenges like evidence collection, legal complexities, political interference, capacity of agencies, and judicial delays. Suggest reforms such as strengthening forensic capabilities, ensuring autonomy of investigative bodies, legal amendments, faster trial mechanisms, and whistleblower protection.

mainsMocks.question: India's push for indigenous semiconductor manufacturing is crucial for its economic growth and national security. Explain the strategic importance for digital economy, defence, and critical infrastructure. Detail government policies like PLI schemes, 'Make in India', and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat'. Evaluate their effectiveness, discussing achievements and remaining challenges such as high capital investment, technological gaps, and human resource development.

mainsMocks.question: Himalayan glaciers are vital water sources for millions, yet they are retreating at an alarming rate. Examine the causes and consequences of this phenomenon and suggest comprehensive strategies for climate change adaptation and water security in the Himalayan region.

GS1.headline: Union Government Launches 'Nari Shakti Utkarsh Yojana' for Women's Economic Empowerment
GS1.explainer:
• The Union Government launched a new flagship scheme, 'Nari Shakti Utkarsh Yojana,' aimed at enhancing women's economic empowerment across the country.
• The scheme focuses on providing skill development, entrepreneurial training, and financial assistance to women, especially in rural and semi-urban areas.
• It integrates existing women-centric programs and introduces new components for digital literacy and market access.
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development will be the nodal agency, collaborating with state governments and NGOs.
• The initiative seeks to boost women's participation in the workforce and foster self-reliance.

GS1.headline: New Report Highlights Alarming Rate of Himalayan Glacier Retreat
GS1.explainer:
• A comprehensive report released by the Ministry of Earth Sciences revealed an alarming acceleration in the retreat of Himalayan glaciers.
• The study, based on satellite imagery and ground observations, indicates significant ice mass loss over the past two decades.
• This rapid melting poses severe threats to water security for millions living downstream and increases the risk of glacial lake outburst floods (GLOFs).
• Scientists attribute the accelerated retreat primarily to rising global temperatures and localized black carbon deposition.
• The report calls for urgent climate action and enhanced regional cooperation for disaster preparedness and adaptation strategies.

Analyze the scientific evidence linking climate change and localized pollutants to glacier melting.
Evaluate the strategies required for climate change adaptation and disaster risk reduction in the Himalayan region.
Examine the importance of international and regional cooperation in addressing transboundary environmental challenges like glacier melt.
GS1.imageDescription: A photograph showing a rapidly melting Himalayan glacier with a prominent glacial lake forming at its base.
GS1.trivia: The Himalayas are often referred to as the 'Third Pole' due to the vast amount of ice and snow they hold, second only to the Arctic and Antarctic regions.

GS1.headline: India Advances Proposal for 'Ancient University of Nalanda' as UNESCO World Heritage Site
GS1.explainer:
• India has submitted an updated nomination dossier to UNESCO for the 'Ancient University of Nalanda' to be inscribed as a World Heritage Site.
• The proposal highlights Nalanda's historical significance as a major Buddhist monastic university and a center of learning in ancient India.
• The updated dossier addresses previous technical queries and provides comprehensive details on the site's Outstanding Universal Value (OUV).
• The Ministry of Culture and Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) are spearheading the efforts to secure this prestigious recognition.
• Inscription would boost cultural tourism, conservation efforts, and international recognition for India's rich heritage.

GS2.headline: Debate Intensifies in Parliament on Potential Uniform Civil Code Implementation
GS2.explainer:
• A vigorous debate took place in both houses of Parliament regarding the potential implementation of a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) across India.
• Members from various parties presented their arguments, focusing on issues of religious freedom, gender justice, and national unity.
• The Law Commission's recent consultations and recommendations were frequently cited during the discussions.
• The government reiterated its commitment to Article 44 of the Constitution, which calls for a UCC, while assuring protection of cultural diversity.
• The discussions highlighted the complex socio-political and legal challenges associated with introducing a UCC.

• A significant part of the agenda was dedicated to enhancing debt relief mechanisms for vulnerable low-income countries.
• India, as a prominent G20 member, emphasized the need for coordinated global action on climate finance and digital public infrastructure.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Directs Stricter Implementation of Environmental Impact Assessments
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, mandated stricter implementation and oversight of Environmental Impact Assessments (EIAs) for developmental projects.
• The court emphasized the need for independent expert committees, public participation, and post-clearance monitoring to ensure environmental sustainability.
• The judgment came in response to a petition highlighting violations and lax enforcement of EIA norms in several industrial and infrastructure projects.
• This ruling aims to strengthen environmental governance and prevent ecological damage caused by unchecked development.
• It underscores the 'polluter pays' principle and the judiciary's role in protecting environmental rights.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5% Amidst Easing Inflation Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive review.
• This decision reflects the MPC's cautious approach, balancing inflation management with supporting economic growth.
• RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das highlighted easing retail inflation but emphasized the need to remain vigilant against potential external shocks.
• The central bank reiterated its commitment to gradually withdrawing accommodation to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Experts project continued stability in lending rates, offering relief to borrowers and aiding investment decisions.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite EOS-07
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched Earth Observation Satellite-07 (EOS-07) from Satish Dhawan Space Centre, Sriharikota.
• EOS-07, weighing approximately 2,200 kg, was launched aboard the GSLV Mk-III rocket, marking another successful mission for ISRO.
• The satellite is equipped with high-resolution imaging sensors for advanced monitoring of agricultural land, forest cover, and urban planning.
• It will also provide crucial data for disaster management, climate change studies, and water resource assessment.
• This launch reinforces India's capabilities in developing and deploying sophisticated space-based Earth observation systems.

GS3.headline: India Unveils Accelerated Targets for National Green Hydrogen Mission
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy announced accelerated targets for the National Green Hydrogen Mission, aiming for faster adoption and production.
• The revised targets include increasing green hydrogen production capacity to 10 million metric tonnes per annum (MMTPA) by 2030.
• Emphasis is placed on developing indigenous manufacturing capabilities for electrolyzers and other components to reduce import dependence.
• The mission aims to attract significant investments, create jobs, and reduce India's reliance on fossil fuels, contributing to energy security.
• New policy incentives and pilot projects in hard-to-abate sectors like steel and fertilizers were also highlighted.

GS3.headline: India Boosts Border Infrastructure Along LAC Amidst Geopolitical Shifts
GS3.explainer:
• India has significantly accelerated the development of critical border infrastructure along the Line of Actual Control (LAC) with China.
• The projects include new roads, bridges, tunnels, and advanced surveillance systems to enhance troop mobility and logistical support.
• This strategic push is in response to evolving geopolitical dynamics and persistent border challenges in the Himalayan region.
• The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) has been instrumental in executing these projects in challenging terrains and extreme weather conditions.
• The enhanced infrastructure aims to bolster India's defensive capabilities and ensure rapid deployment of forces.

GS3.headline: India Achieves Milestone in Domestic Semiconductor Production
GS3.explainer:
• India's efforts to boost indigenous semiconductor manufacturing capabilities have achieved a significant milestone with the inauguration of a new advanced fabrication unit.
• The facility, a joint venture with a leading global chipmaker, marks a crucial step towards reducing India's reliance on imported semiconductors.
• The move is part of the 'Make in India' and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiatives, aiming to establish India as a global hub for electronics manufacturing.
• This development is expected to bolster various sectors, including automotive, consumer electronics, and defence.
• Government incentives and policy support have played a pivotal role in attracting investments and fostering technological advancement.

GS3.headline: New Phase of Clinical Trials Begins for India's Indigenous mRNA Vaccine Candidate
GS3.explainer:
• India's leading biotechnology firm announced the commencement of Phase III clinical trials for its indigenously developed mRNA vaccine candidate.
• This vaccine is designed to target emerging viral strains, showcasing India's growing capabilities in advanced vaccine technology.
• The trials will involve thousands of volunteers across multiple sites, adhering to stringent international safety and efficacy protocols.
• The development is a significant step towards achieving vaccine self-reliance and enhancing public health preparedness against future pandemics.
• Government funding and regulatory support have been crucial in accelerating the research and development process.

GS3.headline: India Records Robust FDI Inflows in Q3, Boosts Manufacturing Sector
GS3.explainer:
• India witnessed robust Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) inflows during the third quarter of the fiscal year 2025-26, signaling investor confidence.
• The manufacturing sector, particularly electronics and automotive, emerged as a major beneficiary, attracting a significant portion of the investments.
• Government's 'Ease of Doing Business' reforms and Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes were cited as key drivers for the increased FDI.
• These inflows are expected to create employment opportunities, foster technological transfers, and boost overall economic growth.
• The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) released the provisional data, highlighting positive investment trends.

GS3.headline: National Water Policy Draft Under Public Consultation for Sustainable Water Management
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti released the draft National Water Policy 2026 for public and stakeholder consultation, aiming for integrated water resource management.
• The new policy emphasizes a shift from water resource development to water resource management, focusing on conservation, efficiency, and equitable distribution.
• Key proposals include promoting water-use efficiency in agriculture, adopting nature-based solutions, and ensuring 'water security' for all.
• It also addresses challenges like inter-state water disputes, groundwater depletion, and the impact of climate change on water availability.
• Feedback from the public and experts will be incorporated to finalize the policy, expected to be a blueprint for India's water future.

GS3.trivia: India's per capita water availability has been steadily declining due to population growth and increased demand, making efficient water management crucial.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils Draft AI Governance Framework for Ethical Development
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) released a draft national framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) governance.
• The framework aims to ensure responsible and ethical development and deployment of AI technologies across various sectors in India.
• Key principles include transparency, accountability, data privacy, fairness, and human-centric design in AI systems.
• It proposes a multi-stakeholder approach involving government, industry, academia, and civil society for policy formulation and oversight.
• The initiative seeks to balance innovation with regulatory safeguards, positioning India as a leader in ethical AI.

GS3.headline: Government Amends E-Waste Management Rules to Promote Circular Economy
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) announced amendments to the E-Waste Management Rules.
• The revised rules aim to strengthen the Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) regime and promote a circular economy for electronic waste.
• Key changes include mandatory targets for producers, incentives for formal recycling, and stricter penalties for non-compliance.
• The amendments also focus on enhancing collection mechanisms and ensuring environmentally sound management of e-waste.
• This move is critical for addressing the growing challenge of electronic waste and conserving valuable resources.

GS3.headline: National Mission for Climate-Resilient Agriculture Records Significant Progress
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare announced significant progress under the National Mission for Sustainable Agriculture (NMSA), particularly its climate-resilient component.
• The mission has successfully implemented climate-smart agricultural practices in several vulnerable regions, benefiting thousands of farmers.
• Focus areas include drought-resistant crop varieties, efficient irrigation techniques, soil health management, and integrated farming systems.
• Capacity building for farmers and adoption of modern technologies are key pillars for enhancing agricultural productivity amidst changing climate patterns.
• The progress underscores India's commitment to ensuring food security and farmers' livelihoods in the face of climate change.

GS3.headline: India Plans Expansion of Strategic Petroleum Reserves to Enhance Energy Security
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas announced plans for a significant expansion of India's Strategic Petroleum Reserves (SPR).
• The move aims to boost the country's crude oil storage capacity, providing a critical buffer against global supply disruptions and price volatility.
• New underground storage facilities are being identified and planned across different regions to enhance strategic depth and accessibility.
• This initiative is part of India's broader energy security strategy, reducing vulnerability to external geopolitical events.
• Public-private partnerships and international collaborations are being explored for financing and technological expertise.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils New Initiatives to Boost Tourism Sector Revival Post-Pandemic
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism launched a new set of initiatives aimed at accelerating the revival and growth of India's tourism sector.
• The programs focus on promoting domestic tourism, enhancing digital infrastructure for tourists, and developing niche tourism products.
• Special emphasis is placed on sustainable and responsible tourism practices, including eco-tourism and cultural heritage trails.
• Financial incentives for hospitality businesses and skill development programs for tourism professionals were also announced.
• The initiatives seek to position India as a premier global tourist destination, contributing significantly to employment and foreign exchange earnings.
